topic: NMP post-1990 acceleration — relabels_standard tag corrected
change_summary: code_analysis.relabels_standard flipped false → true per WIN-036.c3 cross-WIN consistency finding (decider self-applied patch, suggested-patches-2026-05-06T17-19.json).
our_argument_summary: WIN-007 verdict 'Misleading' reads: NMP acceleration around 1990 is real and well-documented, but standard geophysics provides the complete causal mechanism (Livermore et al. 2020 flux-lobe dynamics, DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9). The dome's contribution is a post-hoc label ('aetheric vortex threshold crossing'); no derivation, no advance prediction, no quantitative claim that differs from the standard model. Domain #11 in monitor.py reportedly checks longitude > 130°E against a hardcoded threshold while pole sits at ~139°E — a check that cannot fail. Verdict text and TLDR aligned and accurate.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt:20 — PROS-002 dome registration text ('North Magnetic Pole would show a sharp phase transition around 1990')
raw-text/02-wins.txt confirmation block referencing NOAA NP.xy (pre-1990 0.0466 deg/year linear; post-1990 exponential)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Detail_evidence references NOAA NP.xy time series (drift rate 15 km/yr 1989 → ~60 km/yr 2002; Chulliat & Newitt 2010). The dome's 'check' per our review text is a longitude > 130°E test, with the present pole at ~139°E — automatically passing without testing the dome's actual claim (a phase transition, with order parameter, symmetry breaking, critical scaling). monitor.py is not present in raw-text/ this cycle; we re-verified the description is internally consistent with WIN-036.c3's same description, but cannot independently re-verify monitor.py source against the live repo this run.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The 130°E threshold is a hardcoded scalar; the pole position is fetched live from NOAA NP.xy. Net: a live fetch passed through a hardcoded scalar comparison that any model would pass. Display, not test.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy time-series (NMP position, referenced for trajectory)
Livermore et al. 2020 Nat. Geosci. DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (cited in detail_evidence; not re-verified this cycle)
Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 (cited in detail_evidence; not re-verified this cycle)
Newitt et al. 2002 DOI 10.1186/BF03352065 (cited in detail_evidence; not re-verified this cycle)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None identified. The 'phase transition' framing is the dome's main rhetorical handle; our review correctly flags it as a category error (thermodynamic terminology applied to magnetohydrodynamics).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. WIN-036.c3 noted the Domain #11 monitor.py longitude > 130°E test was characterized in earlier cycles but cannot be independently re-verified without monitor.py in raw-text/. Same caveat applies here. No code claim in detail_evidence has been contradicted by the available raw-text.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Detail_evidence states Domain #11 fetches NOAA NP.xy (live) and compares fetched longitude against a hardcoded scalar (130°E). The fetch is real; the comparison threshold is static. 'live_fetch' is preserved (consistent with WIN-007.c2 and the parallel WIN-036.c2/c3 framing). A defensible alternative reading is 'hardcoded' — the threshold/comparison is what determines pass/fail and that side is static — but the spec emphasises 'fetches live data ... and compares against a prediction', which this does. Keep live_fetch; flag for the build-time monitoring stat that this WIN's 'live' label masks a check that cannot fail.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics already explains the NMP's 1990s acceleration: Livermore et al. 2020 (Nat. Geosci.) attributes it to the elongation/weakening of the Canadian negative flux lobe between 1970–1999 allowing the Siberian lobe to dominate (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence). The dome offers no different numerical prediction — it claims the same NMP trajectory and renames the cause as 'aetheric vortex threshold crossing'. WIN-036, which sits on the SAME NOAA NP.xy data, was already tagged relabels_standard=true (data/wins.json WIN-036 code_analysis); WIN-007 had been false, creating the cross-WIN inconsistency that WIN-036.c3 (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-036.c3.json) flagged. The decider's flip via suggested-patches-2026-05-06T17-19.json is correct on the merits.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Detail_evidence explicitly states the dome's prediction was made post hoc: the 1990s acceleration was documented by Newitt et al. 2002 and Mandea & Dormy 2003 before the dome model existed. The dome adopted the published trajectory and labeled it confirmed. Tag preserved from c2.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun/moon altitudes, aetheric parameters) produces the 1990 timing, the pre-1990 0.0466 deg/year linear rate, or the post-1990 exponential constant. The dome states a label, not an equation. Tag preserved from c2.

holes_found.description: Single-tag fix is correct, but the build-time {{CA_RELABELS}} count in sections.json (data/sections.json — search 'CA_RELABELS' template variable) will silently increment on next build. The previously-stated 'twelve are standard-physics relabellings' (data/sections.json walkthrough block, ~line 159216) is hand-written prose, not template-driven, and may now be off by one. Not blocking, but worth a sweep — either replace the hand-written 'twelve' with a template variable, or just confirm it remains accurate within ±1.
holes_found.recommendation: Spot-check whether the 'twelve are standard-physics relabellings' prose remains accurate after this flip. If not, replace with a template variable so the prose updates with the data.

holes_found.description: Detail_evidence's monitor.py claim ('Domain #11 ... fetches NOAA NP.xy data but only checks whether the current longitude exceeds 130°E') was originally validated against monitor.py in an earlier cycle, but monitor.py is not present in raw-text/ this cycle. The claim has not been falsified; it just cannot be re-verified from the workspace alone. WIN-036.c3 noted the same gap. Not a hole in the WIN, but a hole in our re-verification cycle: if monitor.py changes upstream and we don't pull it, our code claims silently age.
holes_found.recommendation: Pull monitor.py back into raw-text/ at next refresh so code claims can be re-verified.

holes_found.description: Cosmetic. The TLDR-evidence ('competing magnetic flux lobes under Canada and Siberia') simplifies away the directionality (Canadian negative lobe weakening allows Siberian lobe to dominate) that the detail_evidence carries. A non-science reader gets the right gestalt; a working geophysicist would note the simplification doesn't capture the asymmetric mechanism. Within the 'don't split hairs' guidance for TLDR review — flagging only because the underlying mechanism is the load-bearing piece of relabels_standard=true.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider 'competing flux lobes — Siberian lobe taking over from a weakening Canadian lobe' to preserve the asymmetry without adding length.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's PROS-002 wording ('switching from slow linear drift (~0.05 deg/year for 400 years) to rapid exponential acceleration') is itself a quantitative claim — but the dome never DERIVES the 0.05 deg/year pre-1990 rate or the exponential post-1990 form from dome geometry. Our detail_evidence says 'no derivation' but doesn't quote the dome's own quantitative numbers back at it. Quoting the dome's own '0.05 deg/year for 400 years' and pointing out that this is just the historical NOAA NP.xy fit, not a dome derivation, would tighten the rebuttal without adding length.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence — currently says '~15 km/yr in 1989 to ~60 km/yr by 2002' but doesn't tie that back to the dome's own 0.05 deg/year claim

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN: now that WIN-007 and WIN-036 both tag relabels_standard=true, the dashboard's relabel count crosses an interpretive threshold. The WIN-007 ↔ WIN-036 ↔ PROS-002/PROS-006 chain — same NOAA NP.xy data, restated three different ways (acceleration claim, deviation-from-meridian threshold, exponential trajectory) — is a worked example of the 'one phenomenon, multiple WIN entries' inflation pattern Part 2b discusses elsewhere.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies and tracks a real, scientifically significant phenomenon: the NMP did accelerate sharply in the 1990s (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence: '~15 km/yr in 1989 to ~60 km/yr by 2002', citing Chulliat & Newitt 2010, DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001). The acceleration was significant enough that NOAA issued an out-of-cycle WMM update in 2019. Tracking the trajectory and registering a threshold (PROS-002 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:20) is engagement with real data, not random number-picking.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Tracking a published phenomenon is not predicting it. Standard geophysics already attributes the acceleration to flux-lobe dynamics (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence — Livermore et al. 2020, DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, Canadian-vs-Siberian lobe asymmetry). The dome offers no different numerical prediction — same trajectory, renamed cause ('aetheric vortex threshold crossing'). And the underlying observations were public before the dome registered them: Newitt et al. 2002 (DOI 10.1186/BF03352065) and Mandea & Dormy 2003 documented the acceleration before the dome model existed (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence). Pure relabeling of a known, explained, post-hoc-adopted observation.

deeper_analysis:
CYCLE 3 — TAG-FIX VERIFICATION

Queue item 305 asks us to verify the relabels_standard flip from false → true. The flip is correct on the merits and resolves a 30-day-old cross-WIN inconsistency that WIN-036.c2 (2026-04-07) and WIN-036.c3 (2026-05-06T14:48Z) had flagged: WIN-007 and WIN-036 sit on the SAME NOAA NP.xy pole-position data; WIN-036 was tagged relabels_standard=true; WIN-007 had been tagged relabels_standard=false; WIN-007's own detail_evidence said 'standard geophysics provides the complete causal mechanism' — a textbook relabels_standard=true case. The flip aligns the tag with the evidence and with the parallel WIN.

WHAT A DOME DEFENDER MIGHT SAY (advocate-mode steel-man): 'You're calling this a relabel of standard physics, but standard physics did not PREDICT the 1990 timing either — Livermore 2020 explained it after the fact. Both models are post-hoc. Calling the dome a relabel overstates standard physics here.' The reply: the relabels_standard tag is about whether the dome's claimed novel content reduces to renaming the mechanism while pointing at the same data. It does. The fact that standard physics is also post-hoc on this specific timing question is captured by post_hoc=true (which both WIN-007 and WIN-036 carry). Two different tags, two different jobs.

WHAT A DOME DEFENDER MIGHT SAY ON THE TAG SPEC: 'The tag definition says "already predicted AND explained by standard physics" — strict reading requires both. Standard physics didn\'t PREDICT the 1990 timing, only explained it.' The reply: WIN-036 has been carrying relabels_standard=true since c2 without pushback on this strict-conjunction reading. The operating definition has been 'standard physics has a working causal theory; the dome renames'. If we tighten the definition to require pre-prediction, we re-audit ALL relabels_standard=true tags — that's a holistic-review job, not a per-WIN review job. For this run: keep the flip, because consistency with WIN-036 and the rest of the corpus matters more than retroactively re-defining the tag.

VERDICT MISMATCH CHECK: Verdict 'Misleading' continues to match the evidence: real phenomenon, post-hoc adoption, no derivation, false mechanism characterization. No verdict drift. The decider explicitly noted in the patch package that this is a tag-data fix, not a verdict change. Confirmed.

CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY (post-flip):
- WIN-007 (NMP acceleration claim): post_hoc=true, relabels_standard=true, derives_from_dome=false, monitoring=live_fetch ✓
- WIN-036 (NMP deviation >18° from 120°E): post_hoc=true, relabels_standard=true, derives_from_dome=false, monitoring=hardcoded ✓
- Both rely on identical NOAA NP.xy data; both are post-hoc adoptions; both relabel the same standard-physics explanation; neither derives from dome geometry. The only legitimate tag difference is monitoring.

QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION: Pre-1990 0.0466 deg/year × 400 years = 18.64°. Post-1990 ~60 km/yr by 2002 at 80°N translates to ~0.54°/yr (Earth-radius arithmetic) — order-of-magnitude consistent with the published trajectory. Pole at 139.298°E means deviation from 120°E meridian = 19.298° > 18° threshold (WIN-036.c3 spot-check). Numbers are internally consistent.

BUILD-TIME COUNT IMPACT: sections.json relies on {{CA_RELABELS}} template variable. The flip will auto-increment the dashboard count. The hand-written 'twelve are standard-physics relabellings' prose in sections.json (~line 159216) is not template-driven and may now be off by one; flagged as minor hole #1 for the decider's next sweep.

TRIGGER METADATA: This is a priority-queue-driven re-review of a tag correction. The c2 review (2026-04-06T23:45Z) had relabels_standard=false; this c3 review confirms relabels_standard=true is the correct value. No further patch needed — the decider's self-applied fix is bit-perfect.

citations_verified: raw-text/02-wins.txt:20 — PROS-002 dome registration text matches detail_evidence's 'phase transition' summary
raw-text/03-predictions.txt:39-41 — PROS-006 (NMP deviation) cross-references WIN-007's NOAA NP.xy data — confirmed in fresh clone
citations_unchecked: Livermore et al. 2020 Nat. Geosci. DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (cited in detail_evidence — unchecked this cycle; same DOI verified in WIN-036.c3, no contradiction)
Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 (cited in detail_evidence — unchecked this cycle)
Newitt et al. 2002 DOI 10.1186/BF03352065 (cited in detail_evidence — unchecked this cycle)
monitor.py Domain #11 — not present in raw-text/ this cycle (same caveat as WIN-036.c3)

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender's strongest line: 'You\'re calling this a relabel of standard physics, but the dome registered a falsifiable threshold (PROS-006: NMP > 18° east of 120°E meridian) and the threshold has been crossed — that is a confirmed prediction, regardless of whether the dome\'s mechanism is novel. Critique the mechanism if you want; that doesn\'t un-confirm the prediction.' Secondary line: 'Standard physics did not PREDICT the 1990 timing either; Livermore 2020 explained it after the fact. Calling the dome a relabel of standard physics overstates standard physics here.'
advocate_mode.rebuttal: (1) The threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° at the time the dome registered PROS-006 (2026-03-06): WMM2025 had published 139.298°E in December 2024, 14 months earlier (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41; WIN-036.c3 spot-check). 'Confirmed within 6 days' becomes 'confirmed before registration' once the prior data publication is acknowledged. The OTS canary makes this provable: the prediction document and the observation document need to be timestamped independently; here they are not. (2) Yes, standard physics is also post-hoc on the 1990 timing — that's why both WIN-007 and WIN-036 carry post_hoc=true. The relabels_standard tag is about whether the dome's claimed novel content reduces to renaming the mechanism; it does.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: NONE post-flip. The previous WIN-007 vs WIN-036 inconsistency (relabels_standard false vs true) is resolved by this patch. Other tags align: both are post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false. Monitoring differs (live_fetch vs hardcoded) which is correct given the actual code paths.

summary_for_decider: Tag flip relabels_standard=true on WIN-007 is correct on the merits and resolves a 30-day-old cross-WIN inconsistency with WIN-036. Verdict 'Misleading' is unaffected. recommended_action: no_change — patch verified. Two minor follow-ups: (1) the hand-written 'twelve are standard-physics relabellings' prose in sections.json (~line 159216) may now be off by one — sweep or template-ize; (2) WIN-007 detail_evidence's monitor.py Domain #11 claim cannot be re-verified from raw-text/ this cycle (same caveat as WIN-036.c3) — pull monitor.py back into raw-text/ before relying on those code-claims again. No batching contamination from the EXP-289 reviews queued behind this item; this item is structurally distinct (tag fix vs prose substring replace).
